title: Continuity of Insulation
full_text:
3.2.1.2 Continuity of Insulation
Continuity of Insulation
1) Where mechanical ducts and chases or electrical system components, such as
pipes, ducts, conduits, cabinets, panels, or recessed heaters, are placed within and
parallel to the building envelope, the overall thermal transmittance of the building envelope
at the projected area of the mechanical or electrical system components shall not be
increased.
2) Joints between components of the building envelope, such as expansion or
construction joints or joints between walls and doors or fenestration, shall be insulated
in a manner that provides continuity across such joints. (See Note A-3.2.1.2.(2).)

sentences.text: Where mechanical ducts and chases or electrical system components, such as pipes, ducts, conduits, cabinets, panels, or recessed heaters, are placed within and parallel to the building envelope, the overall thermal transmittance of the building envelope at the projected area of the mechanical or electrical system components shall not be increased.

Joints between components of the building envelope, such as expansion or construction joints or joints between walls and doors or fenestration, shall be insulated in a manner that provides continuity across such joints. (See Note A-3.2.1.2.(2).)
